definition: VIX-MOVE correlation structure encodes the dominant macro shock type and is itself regime-switching. Demand shocks produce positive correlation (~0.65 in 2008-09), supply shocks produce decoupling (~0.25-0.35 in 2022). The current weakly-positive state (+0.20 to +0.35) is historically the most unstable transitional regime. When VIX-MOVE are positively correlated, credit spreads widen faster through the funding cost channel.
evidence: Demand shocks produce VIX-MOVE positive correlation (~0.65 in 2008-09)
When VIX-MOVE positively correlated credit spreads widen faster
Confirmed from radically different frameworks in pair_2 debate
open_questions: What are the reliable transition probabilities from the weakly-positive state?
Can the credit-specific diagnostic use be validated with additional filters?
How does the fiscal shock type map to the correlation regime?
Is the causal chain from shock type to correlation regime tight enough for real-time use?
